medications.indication_rationale: Paced sipping of ORS replaces fluid/electrolytes and is absorbed better than plain water during a secretory flare.
medications.dose_route_frequency: sip ~100 mL every 15 minutes as tolerated

medications.indication_rationale: Reduces mucosal secretion in microscopic colitis flares.
Budesonide is a GP/gastroenterology decision for refractory microscopic colitis, not an acute telehealth prescription tonight.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Paced ORS sipping tonight; avoid dairy, caffeine and artificial sweeteners tomorrow; a bland diet.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Optimises absorption and reduces flare severity.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Ask your daughter to check in overnight given you're on your own.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Provides a safety check if dehydration worsens.

patient_education_points.point: This is a flare of your microscopic colitis — the bowel lining is leaking fluid rather than absorbing it. Your urine output is normal and you're thinking clearly, so you're not severely dehydrated right now.
Sip the rehydration solution slowly (about 100 mL every 15 minutes) rather than gulping water, take bismuth, and avoid dairy/caffeine tomorrow.
Because you're on your own, get face-to-face help tonight (urgent care/ED) if you stop passing urine, feel like you'll pass out, become confused, or pass blood/black stools or develop a fever.

follow_up_plan.interval: reassess tonight if worsening; GP within 3-5 days
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Hydration/response, maintenance therapy (e.g. budesonide) for the colitis.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: No urine output, near-syncope, confusion, bloody/black stools, or fever — urgent care/ED.

must_include_items: Gauge dehydration severity by proxy (urine output, postural symptoms, mentation)
Start oral rehydration (paced ORS) and bismuth
Keep a low threshold for same-day face-to-face review (lives alone)
acceptable_alternatives: Same-day face-to-face assessment if dehydration is worsening
errors_of_commission: Reflex ED admission for IV fluids in a mildly dehydrated urine-passing patient without trying ORS
Recommending gulping large volumes of plain water
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2/T3 case: passing requires dehydration assessment + oral rehydration + a low threshold for face-to-face review given she lives alone. Reflex IV-fluids admission or unscreened reassurance are failures.
